requirements:
Implement role-based authentication and authorization for at least these roles: Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Administrative Staff, and Student.
Allow the Administrator to create, edit, and delete teaching modules.
Allow the Administrator to associate one or more referring teachers with a teaching module and to modify those associations.
Provide a global user database (teachers and students) managed by the Administrator, supporting create, read, update, and delete operations on user records.
Allow the Administrator to assign teachers and students to modules (enroll/unenroll users) from the global database.
Allow a Referring Teacher to add existing teachers and existing students (already present in the global database) to their module(s).
Enable a Teacher to record an absence for a student in a specific module and to include an optional textual comment with the absence record.
Record metadata for each absence entry: reporter (user who recorded it), date/time, module, student, comment, and current justification status.
Allow Teachers to view a list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach.
Allow Administrative Staff to view all student absences across the system and to enter or update the reason and justification status for any absence.
Support grouping and filtering of absence records by module and by student.
Support filtering and reporting based on absence justification status (e.g., justified, unjustified, pending).
Provide an alerts page for teaching staff that lists students who have more than three unjustified absences (threshold configurable by Administrator).

Provide role-based authentication and authorization for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff accounts.
Provide a portal dashboard per role that exposes only the functions and data allowed for that role.
Allow the Administrator to create, modify, and delete teaching modules.
Allow the Administrator to associate each module with one or more referring teachers and update those associations.
Maintain a global database of teachers and students that the Administrator can create, update, deactivate, and delete.
Allow a Referring Teacher to add (assign) existing registered teachers and students from the global database to their modules.
Allow Teachers to record an absence for a student in a module they teach and to include an optional comment when reporting the absence.
Record for each absence: student, module, reporting teacher, date/time, and any teacher comment.
Allow Administrative Staff to enter or update the reason for an absence and to mark an absence as justified or unjustified.
Grant Administrative Staff read access to all student absence records and the ability to search and filter them.
Allow Teachers to view the list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach, with filtering by module, student, date range, and justification status.
Support grouping and viewing absence records by module and by student.
Allow Students to submit a justification request for an absence (including comments and optional supporting evidence) to be reviewed by Administrative Staff.
Allow Administrative Staff to review student justifications, accept or reject them, and record the decision and rationale on the absence record.
Provide a page/dashboard that alerts teaching staff to students who have more than three unjustified absences, and ensure the alert list is updated automatically.
Maintain an audit trail of all absence-related actions (creation, edits, justification submissions, status changes) including user and timestamp.

The system shall support user authentication and role-based access control for at least the roles: Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Administrative Staff, and Student.
The Administrator shall be able to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) teaching modules.
The Administrator shall be able to assign one or more referring teachers to a module and update or remove those associations.
The Administrator shall manage a global user database of teachers and students (CRUD on user records) and group users as needed.
Referring Teachers shall be able to add existing registered teachers and students from the global database to their module(s).
The system shall allow a teacher to record an absence for a student in a specific module, specifying date/time, absent student, reporting teacher, and allowing an optional comment.
The system shall record metadata for each absence: module, student, date/time, reporting teacher, comment, and justification status/reason.
Teachers shall be able to view a list of all absences for every student enrolled in any module they teach.
Administrative Staff shall be able to view all recorded student absences across modules and students.
Administrative Staff shall be able to enter or update the official reason/justification for any recorded absence and change the absence justification status.
The system shall provide views/reports that group and present absences by module and by student.
The system shall provide a page accessible to teaching staff that lists students who have more than three unjustified absences and highlights these cases.
Students shall be able to view their own absence records and submit a justification request to Administrative Staff for an absence.
The system shall maintain an audit trail for absence records and changes (who recorded/edited an absence, who entered justification, and timestamps).
The system shall prevent adding users to modules unless the users already exist in the global database (referring teachers may only add registered users).

Support four user roles with authentication and role-based access control: Administrator, Administrative Staff, Teacher, and Student.
Allow the Administrator to create, update, and delete teaching modules.
Allow the Administrator to assign one or more referring teachers to each teaching module and change those associations.
Provide a global user database managed by the Administrator containing teacher and student records, with create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations.
Allow referring teachers to add (assign/enroll) existing teachers and existing students from the global database to their modules.
Allow a Teacher to record an absence for a student in a specific module and to attach an optional comment to that absence entry.
Allow Teachers to view the list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach.
Allow Administrative Staff to view all student absences across the system.
Allow Administrative Staff to enter or update the reason/status for any recorded absence (e.g., justified, unjustified, pending).
Store for each absence: student, module, date/time, reporting teacher, comment, administrative reason/status, and modification history.
Provide views/reports that group and display absences by module and by student.
Provide a page or view that alerts teaching staff to students who have more than three unjustified absences.
Allow Students to view their own absence records and current justification/status for each absence.
Provide search and lookup functionality so users (teachers/administrators) can find existing students and teachers in the global database when assigning them to modules.

Provide role-based user accounts and authentication for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Administrative Staff, and Student.
Allow the Administrator to create, update, and delete teaching modules.
Allow the Administrator to associate one or more referring teachers with each teaching module and to change those associations.
Maintain a global database of teachers and students that the Administrator can create, update, delete, and view.
Allow the Administrator to group teachers and students (e.g., cohorts, classes) and manage those groups.
Allow a Referring Teacher to add existing registered teachers and students to their module(s) (i.e., enroll users in a module) if those users already exist in the global database.
Allow Teachers to record an absence for a student in a specific module and to attach an optional comment to that absence record.
Allow Teachers to view a list of absences for all students enrolled in any module for which they are assigned/authorized.
Allow Administrative Staff to view all student absences across the system and to enter or edit the administrative reason/justification for any absence.
Track and store the status of each absence (e.g., unjustified, justified) along with teacher comments, administrative reasons, timestamps, and the identity of the user who recorded/modified it.
Provide views and reporting that group and filter absences by module and by student (with date-range and sorting options).
Provide a dedicated alert page that lists students with more than three unjustified absences and make that page accessible to teaching staff (and Administrators).
Restrict data access according to role: teachers may see only absences for students in their modules, administrative staff and administrators may see all absences, and students may view their own absence records and justifications.
Maintain an audit log of absence-related actions (creation, modification, justification) including user, action, and timestamp.

The system shall provide secure user authentication for all roles (Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, Administrative Staff).
The system shall support role-based access control that enforces permissions for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.
The Administrator shall be able to create, edit, deactivate, reactivate, and delete user accounts for all roles.
The system shall require unique identifiers for each user (e.g., student ID, teacher ID) and shall prevent duplicate user accounts.
The system shall allow Administrators to import and export users in bulk via common formats (CSV, XLSX) with validation and error reporting.
The system shall allow Administrators to assign one or more roles to a user and to change user roles.
The system shall allow Administrators to reset user passwords and to configure password policies (minimum length, complexity, expiration).
The system shall support password recovery (password reset via secure email link) and optional multi-factor authentication for all users.
The system shall lock accounts after a configurable number of failed login attempts and shall provide an unlock mechanism for Administrators.
The Administrator shall be able to create, edit, and delete teaching modules, including module name, code, description, schedule, and capacity.
The system shall allow a teaching module to be associated with one or more Referring Teachers.
The Administrator shall be able to assign and reassign Referring Teachers to modules and to view current associations.
The Administrator shall be able to associate Teachers and Students with modules and to manage enrollments.
The system shall enforce referential integrity so that modules cannot reference non-existent users.
The global user database shall store and manage Teacher and Student profiles with contact details, enrollment history, and role history.
The Referring Teacher shall be able to add Teachers and Students to a module only if those Teachers and Students already exist in the global database.
The system shall provide a search interface for Referring Teachers to find existing Teachers and Students in the global database before adding them.
If a Referring Teacher attempts to add a Teacher or Student not present in the database, the system shall provide a clear error and an option to request that the Administrator create the user account.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to request bulk additions of module enrollments that Administrators can approve or reject.
The system shall record and display the full list of Teachers and Students grouped by module.
The system shall allow Teachers to report absences for students enrolled in their modules and for themselves in their scheduled sessions.
When a Teacher reports an absence in a module, the system shall allow the Teacher to add a free-text comment limited to a configurable length.
The system shall validate that an absence reported by a Teacher pertains to a scheduled session of the specified module and to students enrolled in that module.
The system shall prevent duplicate absences for the same student, same module, same session (date/time) by the same or different Teachers, and shall provide options to merge or mark duplicates.
Teachers shall be able to mark the type of absence (single session absence, partial session, late arrival, early departure) and duration (start time, end time).
The system shall allow Teachers to mark multiple students absent in a single operation (bulk absence marking).
Teachers shall be able to view and filter the list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach, with filters by date range, status, module, student, and teacher.
The system shall present absence records including date/time, module, reporting Teacher, comment, status (unjustified/justified/pending), and any attached justification documents.
Administrative Staff shall have access to all student absences across the institution, with filters by module, student, date range, status, and Referring Teacher.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter the reason for an absence and to set the justification status (justified, unjustified, pending).
Administrative Staff shall be able to attach documents (e.g., medical certificates) to an absence record and the system shall validate file types and sizes.
The system shall maintain an auditable history of all changes to absence records including who made the change and timestamps.
Absences shall be groupable and viewable by module and by student, with aggregated counts and details.
The system shall provide a dashboard for Teachers showing recent absences in their modules and alerts for students who exceed configured thresholds.
The system shall provide a dashboard for Referring Teachers showing module enrollments, recent absences, and pending actions.
The system shall provide a dashboard for Administrative Staff showing institution-wide absence statistics, pending justifications, and alerts for policy violations.
The system shall send notifications (email and/or in-app) to Students when an absence is recorded for them, including details and any Teacher comments.
The system shall send notifications to Teachers and Referring Teachers when Administrative Staff changes the justification status of an absence.
The system shall send notifications to Administrative Staff when Teachers report absences that require administrative review.
The system shall send alerts to teaching staff (Teachers and Referring Teachers) listing students who have more than three unjustified absences, and this threshold shall be configurable by Administrators.
Alerts for students exceeding unjustified absence thresholds shall be available on a dedicated page and optionally sent via email at configurable intervals (real-time, daily, weekly).
The system shall allow per-student tracking of the number of justified and unjustified absences and shall make the counts available to authorized roles.
Students shall be able to view their own absence records, including dates, modules, reporting Teacher, comments, and justification status.
The system shall allow Students to submit justifications for their recorded absences, including free-text reason and optional attachments, within a configurable time window.
The system shall track submission timestamps for student justifications and enforce the justification deadline configured by Administrators.
Administrative Staff shall be able to approve, reject, or request more information for student-submitted justifications, with automated notifications of the decision to the Student and relevant Teachers.
The system shall maintain a workflow status for each absence (reported, justification_submitted, admin_review, justified, unjustified, appealed) and permit transitions defined by Administrators.
The system shall allow Students to appeal unjustified decisions with supplementary documents; Administrative Staff shall be able to process and record appeal outcomes.
The system shall provide role-appropriate views so Teachers can see which absences are justified/unjustified and any administrative notes.
The system shall provide the ability to export absence lists and aggregated reports by module, student, teacher, date range, and status in CSV and PDF formats.
The system shall provide printable views for modules, attendance sheets, and absence reports.
The system shall allow Administrators to configure absence policies, including thresholds for alerts, justification deadlines, allowed attachment types, and retention periods.
The system shall enforce data validation on all input fields (dates, times, IDs, email addresses) and display clear validation messages.
The system shall record the date and time zone of each absence entry and display dates/times in the user’s preferred time zone.
The system shall allow Administrators to deactivate modules and define behavior for existing absence and enrollment records (archive, cascade delete, or prevent deletion).
The system shall prevent deletion of users or modules if associated records (e.g., absences) would be orphaned, unless an Administrator explicitly confirms archival or deletion and records that action in an audit log.
The system shall provide an audit log of all administrative actions (user creation, role assignment, module creation, deletion, justification decisions) that is searchable and exportable.
The system shall provide fine-grained search and filtering for users, modules, and absences with support for pagination and sorting.
The system shall handle concurrent edits safely, providing optimistic locking or edit conflict resolution when multiple users attempt to modify the same absence, enrollment, or module.
The system shall debounce bulk operations and provide progress and success/failure reporting for long-running imports or exports.
The system shall provide API endpoints (secure, authenticated) for integration with external systems (student information system, calendar, email) and support rate limiting and API key management.
The system shall integrate with calendar systems (e.g., iCal, Google Calendar) to import module schedules and to allow export of relevant sessions.
The system shall validate consistency between module schedules and reported absences (e.g., prevent reporting for non-existent sessions unless explicitly allowed with justification).
The system shall provide role-based configurable automated reminders to students to submit justifications before expiration of the allowable period.
The system shall provide a configurable threshold for late-justifications and mark them as late in the record.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to add administrative-only comments on absence records that are visible to authorized staff but not necessarily to students.
The system shall provide localization support and allow content and UI labels to be translated; the default language shall be configurable by Administrators.
The system shall be responsive and usable on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices and support modern browsers.
The system shall comply with applicable data protection and privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR) including consent management, data access requests, and right to be forgotten workflows for personal data.
The system shall encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit using industry-standard algorithms (e.g., TLS for transport, AES for storage).
The system shall enforce that only authorized roles can view personally identifiable information and sensitive documents, using role-based permissions and access controls.
The system shall provide secure file storage for attachments with virus/malware scanning and configurable maximum file sizes.
The system shall deliver email notifications reliably with retry logic and provide status logs for notification delivery and failures.
The system shall guarantee response times of under 2 seconds for standard UI queries under expected load and shall degrade gracefully under heavy load.
The system shall be able to handle concurrent usage from a configurable number of users (e.g., initial target 5,000 concurrent sessions) and be scalable horizontally.
The system shall provide at least 99.5% availability (uptime) excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
The system shall provide daily backups of databases and stored documents and allow restoration to a point-in-time within a configurable retention period.
The system shall provide monitoring, alerting, and logs for system health, errors, performance metrics, and security incidents.
The system shall implement input sanitization and protection against common web vulnerabilities (e.g., XSS, CSRF, SQL injection).
The system shall require secure session management with configurable session timeout and protection against session fixation.
The system shall allow Administrators to configure retention and archival policies for absence records (e.g., archive records older than X years) and to purge data per legal requirements.
The system shall enforce business rules such as preventing Teachers from reporting absences for students not enrolled, preventing Students from submitting justifications after deadline unless a late-justification workflow is triggered, and preventing duplication.
The system shall provide comprehensive role-based help and documentation accessible in-app (user guides for Administrators, Teachers, Referring Teachers, Administrative Staff, and Students).
The system shall provide real-time indicators and confirmations for user actions (e.g., absence saved, justification submitted) and shall present meaningful error messages.
The system shall provide PDF/CSV export templates for compliance and audit use and shall include metadata (exported by, timestamp, filters applied).
The system shall enable Administrators to configure email templates and in-app notification messages with variables for personalization.
The system shall provide a configurable alerting page that can be filtered by module, Referring Teacher, or student group and that highlights students above the unjustified-absence threshold.
The system shall provide metrics and charts for absence trends over time (by module, student cohort, Teacher) and enable exporting these analytics.
The system shall ensure time zone consistency for multi-campus or remote users and shall provide options to view times in local or campus time.
The system shall provide a secure method for external Referring Teachers or staff who are not regular users to request user creation or report attendance exceptions, with audit trails.
The system shall provide data validation and business-rule checking on imports and shall present a report of import errors and warnings for Administrator review.
The system shall ensure actions that affect many records (bulk delete, bulk justify/unjustify) require confirmation and shall be reversible via undo within a configurable window or via an administrative audit-based restore.
The system shall keep versioned history of critical records (absence entries, justification decisions, module assignments) and allow Administrators to view prior versions.
The system shall support configurable business hours and holiday calendars to suppress false absence alerts for scheduled non-instructional days.
The system shall allow mapping of Teachers to substitute Teachers and shall support reporting absences by substitute Teachers with clear attribution.
The system shall provide an API or UI action for Teachers to mark an entire session as canceled (e.g., class canceled) and to convert pending absences accordingly with notifications.
The system shall enforce data ownership and exportability so that Students and Trainers can obtain a copy of their records upon request.
The system shall perform input length limits, character encoding handling, and reject invalid characters in names and free-text fields to prevent data corruption.
The system shall provide role-specific reports exportable on demand and scheduled periodic reports (daily/weekly/monthly) deliverable via email or available in the UI.
The system shall support localization of date and time formats, number formats, and currency where used.
The system shall ensure accessibility conformance to WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines for users with disabilities.
The system shall provide clear workflows and data protections for sensitive situations (e.g., minors) including parental contact permissions and guardianship data fields managed by Administrators.
The system shall provide configurable retention of notification history for compliance and auditing.
The system shall provide an administrator-configurable policy for what constitutes an "unjustified" absence and how corrective actions or disciplinary workflows are triggered.
The system shall allow linking of absence records to disciplinary records or student support cases, with access limited to authorized Administrative Staff.
The system shall log and alert on suspicious activity (e.g., mass deletion, frequent failed logins) and provide incident response workflows.
The system shall provide an exportable list of students exceeding unjustified absence thresholds, including module context and dates.
The system shall provide the ability to tag or categorize absences (e.g., medical, personal, excused) and allow Administrators to manage tag lists.
The system shall include client-side and server-side validation to ensure consistent enforcement of rules and to prevent circumvention.
The system shall include automated tests and unit/integration test coverage for core workflows (user management, module management, absence recording, justification workflow) as part of delivery.
The system shall provide administrative impersonation capability (audit logged) so Administrators can view the system exactly as a given user to troubleshoot, with strict controls and logging.
The system shall maintain an SLA for support response times for critical incidents and shall provide a contact channel for system support.
The system shall provide mechanisms to archive historical modules and cohorts while maintaining linked absence records accessible in read-only mode.
The system shall allow configuration of who receives which types of notifications (global and per-module overrides), and allow users to set personal notification preferences where appropriate.
The system shall ensure that deletion or anonymization of a student for privacy reasons preserves aggregated statistical data unless legally required to purge.
The system shall provide a sandbox or staging environment that mirrors production for testing imports, bulk operations, and policy configuration before applying changes to live data.
The system shall provide consistent timezone-aware scheduling for sessions and allow Administrators to correct misreported session associations if teachers misattribute an absence to a wrong session.
The system shall include rate limiting and monitoring on public endpoints (e.g., login, API) to mitigate abuse and DDoS threats.
The system shall implement a clear error reporting mechanism to capture uncaught exceptions and provide user-friendly error pages with reference codes for support.
The system shall provide a migration and data conversion plan and tools for onboarding historical attendance records into the system with provenance preserved.

The system shall provide secure user accounts for five roles: Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.
The system shall require authentication for all users before accessing any protected functionality.
The system shall support role-based access control that enforces permissions for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.
The system shall allow Administrators to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) teaching modules.
The system shall allow Administrators to associate one or more Referring Teachers with each teaching module.
The system shall allow Administrators to assign multiple Teachers to a teaching module.
The system shall allow Administrators to add, edit, and remove users (students, teachers, referring teachers, administrative staff) in the global user database.
The system shall allow Administrators to manage user attributes including name, email, student/employee ID, role, contact details, enrollment status, and metadata.
The system shall allow Administrators to enable, disable, or soft-delete user accounts while preserving historical attendance records.
The system shall enforce unique identifiers (e.g., student ID or email) to prevent duplicate user accounts.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add existing registered Teachers to modules, with a search or lookup of the global user database.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add existing registered Students to modules, with a search or lookup of the global user database.
The system shall prevent Referring Teachers from creating new user accounts; they may only link existing registered users to modules.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to view and manage the roster (list of students and teachers) of modules they refer.
The system shall allow Teachers to record an absence event for a student in a module they teach.
The system shall allow Teachers to record an absence event for themselves in a module they teach.
The system shall record the date and time (timestamp) and the session or class occurrence when an absence is recorded.
The system shall allow Teachers to add an optional textual comment when reporting an absence.
The system shall allow Teachers to indicate the type of absence (e.g., full session absence, partial absence, late) when recording an absence.
The system shall enforce validation to prevent recording an absence outside the scheduled session times unless explicitly allowed with justification.
The system shall prevent duplicate absence records for the same student, module, and session unless an override is explicitly performed with an audit record.
The system shall provide Teachers with a view that lists all absences for students enrolled in modules the Teacher is assigned to.
The system shall allow Teachers to filter the absence list by module, student, date range, absence type, and justification status.
The system shall allow Teachers to sort and paginate the absence list.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to access and view all student absences across all modules.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter or edit the official reason for an absence and mark an absence as justified or unjustified.
The system shall maintain a status field for each absence with states including: Recorded, Pending Justification, Justified, Unjustified, Reviewed, and Archived.
The system shall allow Students to view their own absence records with associated comments and status.
The system shall allow Students to submit justifications for their absences, including textual explanation and file attachments (e.g., medical certificates).
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to view submitted justification documents and accept, reject, or request more information.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to view submitted student justifications and the resulting administrative decision.
The system shall support upload of justification documents with file type and size restrictions enforced (configurable allowed types and maximum size).
The system shall scan uploaded files for viruses and malware before storing.
The system shall keep an immutable audit trail for all changes to absence records, including who made the change, timestamp, and previous values.
The system shall allow authorized users to edit absence records only where permitted by role and only with the change recorded in the audit trail.
The system shall allow authorized users to delete absence records only as soft-deletes with preserved history and audit markers.
The system shall provide an alerts page that lists students with more than three (>) unjustified absences.
The system shall allow configuration of the threshold that triggers the “excessive unjustified absences” alert.
The system shall provide the alerts page to Referring Teachers and Teachers assigned to the affected students and to Administrators and Administrative Staff.
The system shall allow filtering and sorting within the alerts page by module, class, student, number of unjustified absences, and date range.
The system shall allow exporting alerts and absence lists to CSV and PDF formats.
The system shall group absence records by module and by student in views and reports, allowing the user to toggle grouping.
The system shall provide aggregated reports showing absence counts per student, per module, per teacher, and per time period.
The system shall provide trend charts and summary statistics for absences over time (configurable date ranges).
The system shall support bulk operations including bulk import of users, bulk assignment of students to modules, bulk marking of absences, and bulk justification processing.
The system shall validate bulk imports for format errors, duplicates, referential integrity, and provide a preview and error report before applying.
The system shall prevent assignment of a user to a module if the user does not exist in the global database, and shall provide an explicit error message.
The system shall allow Administrators to define module schedules (days, times, academic periods) and link absences to scheduled sessions.
The system shall allow Teachers to record absences retrospectively with an indication that the absence was recorded late, and the system shall record the recording timestamp and reason for late recording.
The system shall implement business rules to determine whether a justification submitted after a configurable time window is acceptable.
The system shall notify Students when an absence has been recorded for them.
The system shall notify Teachers and Referring Teachers when a student in their module reaches the alert threshold of unjustified absences.
The system shall notify Administrative Staff when a justification is submitted by a student.
The system shall support configurable notification channels including in-app notifications and email, and optionally SMS.
The system shall allow users to opt in/out of email or SMS notifications where permitted by policy.
The system shall provide real-time or near-real-time notifications for critical events (absence recorded, justification submitted, threshold reached).
The system shall provide a dashboard for each role summarizing relevant data: Administrators (system stats, unresolved justifications), Referring Teachers (module rosters, alerts), Teachers (today’s sessions and absences), Students (their absence history and pending actions), Administrative Staff (pending justifications and unresolved cases).
The system shall provide search functionality to find students, teachers, modules, and absence records by ID, name, module code, and other attributes.
The system shall support pagination, sorting, and server-side filtering for large result sets.
The system shall provide an activity log for Administrative Staff and Administrators showing recent system actions, errors, and key events.
The system shall provide APIs (RESTful or GraphQL) for integration with external Student Information Systems, authentication providers, and reporting tools.
The system shall support single sign-on (SSO) via SAML 2.0 and OAuth2/OIDC integrations.
The system shall require strong password policies including minimum length, complexity, expiration policy (configurable), and account lockout after configurable failed attempts.
The system shall support two-factor authentication (2FA) for Administrator and Administrative Staff accounts.
The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2+ and sensitive personal data at rest using strong encryption.
The system shall restrict access to justification documents and personal data to authorized roles only.
The system shall support data export and subject data requests for compliance (e.g., download all personal data for a student).
The system shall support data deletion workflows to comply with privacy regulations, including soft-delete and secure purge after retention period.
The system shall allow configuration of data retention periods for attendance records, justifications, and files.
The system shall log all authentication attempts, successful logins, failed logins, and account changes with timestamps and source IPs.
The system shall provide protection against common web vulnerabilities (CSRF, XSS, SQL injection) and validate all inputs server-side and client-side.
The system shall implement rate limiting for critical endpoints to prevent abuse and denial-of-service.
The system shall implement role-based UI elements so users only see features they are authorized to access.
The system shall ensure that Teachers can only view absence records and students for modules to which they are assigned.
The system shall ensure that Referring Teachers can only add/link users to modules they refer and can only view rosters and absences for modules they refer.
The system shall ensure that Students can only see their own records and not other students’ personal information.
The system shall provide internationalization (i18n) support and configurable language options for the user interface.
The system shall be responsive and usable on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
The system shall meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards for critical screens and workflows.
The system shall provide clear user feedback for success, validation errors, and system errors with actionable guidance.
The system shall provide contextual help, tooltips, and a help center or documentation for common tasks.
The system shall provide undo/confirm dialogs for destructive actions (e.g., deleting modules, removing students from modules).
The system shall support transactional integrity so that multi-step operations (e.g., bulk assignments) are atomic or present a clear partial failure state with rollback options.
The system shall support scalable architecture capable of handling concurrent users in the expected deployment size (configurable target e.g., thousands of users) and allow horizontal scaling.
The system shall provide average page response times under normal load of less than 2 seconds for primary user flows.
The system shall support scheduled backups of databases and file storage with a recoverability target (configurable RTO/RPO, e.g., RTO 4 hours, RPO 1 hour).
The system shall provide monitoring and alerting for system health metrics (CPU, memory, disk, error rates), and send alerts to administrators on critical failures.
The system shall have an uptime objective appropriate for educational operations (e.g., 99.9% monthly availability) and planned maintenance windows.
The system shall provide import/export audit reports for all bulk operations showing successes, failures, and row-level error messages.
The system shall validate all date/time inputs against the system calendar and the module schedule timezone and prevent inconsistent timezone-related errors.
The system shall support configurable academic periods/semesters and associate modules and absences with a specific academic period.
The system shall allow Administrators to configure absence types, statuses, and business rules without code changes.
The system shall implement input validation rules for textual comments (length limits, prohibited content filtering) and provide character limits.
The system shall enforce file attachment quotas per user and per module to prevent excessive storage consumption.
The system shall provide secure storage for uploaded documents with per-file access controls and the ability to revoke access.
The system shall ensure referential integrity so that deletion of modules, users, or roles is blocked or cascaded according to configured policies and accompanied by notifications and audit records.
The system shall preserve historical attendance and justification records even if a user changes role or is unassigned from a module.
The system shall provide role-specific audit reports listing who performed approvals, justifications, edits, and deletions for compliance reviews.
The system shall allow Administrators to restore soft-deleted users, modules, and absence records within a configurable restoration window.
The system shall provide exportable weekly and monthly reports for Administrative Staff showing unresolved justifications and students approaching disciplinary thresholds.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to add internal notes to student records visible only to authorized staff.
The system shall prevent Teachers from modifying administrative decisions (e.g., re-justifying an absence marked unjustified by Administrative Staff) unless Administrator privileges are used, with changes audited.
The system shall support timezone-aware scheduling so users in different timezones see consistent session times.
The system shall provide confirmation receipts to Students and Teachers when justification documents are submitted or processed.
The system shall include role-based dashboards showing counts of pending actions (pending justifications, unresolved alerts) and links to relevant lists.
The system shall support scheduling automated reports and alerts to be delivered to configured email lists or groups on a schedule.
The system shall provide a secure administrator console for system configuration, user management, threshold settings, and audit access.
The system shall require multi-tenancy controls if used by multiple institutions, ensuring strict data segregation per tenant.
The system shall provide a test/staging environment that mirrors production for safe testing of updates and bulk operations.
The system shall be implemented with automated unit tests, integration tests, and acceptance tests covering core workflows for continuous integration.
The system shall undergo regular security assessments and penetration testing, with remediation tracking.
The system shall provide versioning for exported reports and be able to reproduce historical views of alerts and absence counts for audit dates.
The system shall enforce minimum and maximum limits on the number of modules a teacher can be assigned to (configurable) and validate assignments.
The system shall provide a configurable grace period for justifications submitted after an absence before the absence is automatically marked unjustified.
The system shall provide conflict detection and resolution when two users attempt to update the same absence record concurrently.
The system shall display the current status of the module rosters and last updated timestamps for transparency.
The system shall maintain a changelog of module associations when modules have their referring teachers updated, including previous associations.
The system shall notify affected Teachers and Referring Teachers when an Administrator modifies module-teacher associations.
The system shall provide a mechanism for students to report errors in their attendance records and for staff to track and resolve these disputes.
The system shall enforce legal and policy constraints for minors, such as parental notification and consent workflows, where applicable and configurable.
The system shall provide anonymized aggregate data for reporting and research without exposing personally identifiable information.
The system shall implement graceful error handling with logging and user-friendly messages and provide an error reporting mechanism to administrators.
The system shall provide time-limited access tokens for API access and support revocation of tokens.
The system shall provide capability to archive historical modules and associated attendance data and to restore them when needed.
The system shall allow configurable business rules to automatically escalate students to administrative review when threshold conditions are met (e.g., >3 unjustified absences).
The system shall maintain a mapping of modules to academic courses and classes for hierarchical reporting.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to bulk-update absence statuses (e.g., mark multiple absences as justified) with audit trails and validation.
The system shall allow Administrators to export the entire global database (users, modules, enrollments, absences) in a machine-readable format for archival or migration.
The system shall provide scheduled maintenance notifications and display maintenance windows in the UI for end-users.
The system shall support localization of date and time formats and number formats according to user preferences.
The system shall provide clear escalation paths and contact information for support within the application.
The system shall support configurable retention and deletion policies for audit logs and monitoring data to balance compliance and storage costs.
The system shall allow Admins to configure and enforce a policy for maximum allowed unjustified absences before automated actions (notifications, escalations, flags) are taken.
The system shall provide filtering to identify students with patterns of partial absences or tardiness and generate alerts accordingly.
The system shall ensure all date and time-based calculations (e.g., counting absences toward threshold) are auditable and reproducible.
The system shall provide metrics on system usage (logins, daily active users, absences recorded per day) for administrators.
The system shall support a consent mechanism for collecting and storing personal data where required by law and record consent events.
The system shall provide version-controlled release notes and change logs for all functional changes visible to administrators.
The system shall include an onboarding workflow for new Administrators and Referring Teachers to learn how to add/link users and configure modules.
The system shall offer configurable retention of justification attachments and the ability for Administrators to purge attachments manually when required.

The system shall provide role-based authentication for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff accounts.
The system shall support secure user registration with email verification and administrator approval workflows.
The system shall allow Administrators to create, read, update, and delete teaching modules.
The system shall allow Administrators to associate one or more Referring Teachers to each teaching module.
The system shall allow Administrators to assign Teachers and Referring Teachers to modules and to remove those assignments.
The system shall maintain a global database of all Teachers and Students that Administrators can create, read, update, and delete.
The system shall allow Administrators to import and export Teacher and Student records in common formats (CSV and XLSX).
The system shall require unique identifiers (e.g., student ID) for all student and teacher records and validate uniqueness on create and import.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add Teachers and Students to modules only if those user records already exist in the global database.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to request creation of new Teacher or Student records when a requested record does not exist, subject to Administrator approval.
The system shall allow Teachers assigned to a module to record an absence for a student for a specific module session.
The system shall allow Teachers to record absence type categories including absent, late, left early, partial absence, and other custom types.
The system shall require Teachers to enter the date and time (or session identifier) when recording an absence and validate that the timestamp is not inconsistent with the module schedule.
The system shall allow Teachers to add an optional free-text comment when recording an absence.
The system shall prevent duplicate absence records for the same student, module, and session and shall flag potential duplicates for review.
The system shall allow Teachers to view a list of all absences for students enrolled in any module to which they are assigned.
The system shall allow Teachers to filter and search the absence list by student name, student ID, module, date range, absence type, and justification status.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to view and search all student absences across all modules without restriction.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter a reason for an absence and associate supporting documentation (file upload) to the absence record.
The system shall allow Students to submit a justification request for an absence, including a textual explanation and file attachments (e.g., medical certificate).
The system shall track the justification lifecycle states for each absence (e.g., pending, justified, rejected, waived) and record the user and timestamp for each state change.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff and Administrators to accept, reject, or modify student justifications and to add administrative reasons and comments.
The system shall automatically mark an absence as unjustified by default until an Administrative Staff or Administrator explicitly justifies it.
The system shall provide configurable justification thresholds and rules (e.g., certain absence types auto-justify or require documentation) configurable by Administrators.
The system shall group absences by module and by student for viewing and reporting.
The system shall display a dedicated alert page for Teaching Staff that lists students with more than three unjustified absences and allow filtering by module, period, and cohort.
The system shall allow Administrators to configure the unjustified-absence alert threshold per module and globally.
The system shall send configurable notifications (email and optional in-app) to Students, Teachers, Referring Teachers, and Administrative Staff when key events occur (absence recorded, justification submitted, justification approved/rejected, alert threshold exceeded).
The system shall allow users to opt into or out of notification channels subject to institution policy.
The system shall maintain a complete audit log of all actions (creation, modification, deletion, approvals) capturing actor, role, timestamp, and before/after values.
The system shall provide Administrators and Administrative Staff with bulk operations for justifying, rejecting, exporting, and deleting absence records with confirmation and audit logging.
The system shall allow export of absence reports by module, student, teacher, date range, and justification status to CSV and PDF formats.
The system shall provide dashboards showing aggregate statistics such as total absences, unjustified absences, absences per module, absences per student, and trend charts over time.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to see dashboards limited to modules and students they are authorized to view.
The system shall support pagination and efficient server-side filtering for large absence datasets.
The system shall allow Administrators to define and manage cohorts, classes, or groups of students and to enroll or remove students from modules in bulk.
The system shall validate that a Teacher recording an absence is authorized for the specified module and return an error if not authorized.
The system shall allow retroactive entry and editing of absences with mandatory justification for late entries, and shall log who made the change and why.
The system shall include a confirmation step and undo option for destructive actions (e.g., deletion of absences or user accounts) and retain deleted records in a recoverable archive for a configurable retention period.
The system shall support attachment uploads with allowed file type restrictions, file size limits, virus scanning, and encrypted storage.
The system shall enforce input validation on all forms including required fields, valid date/time formats, file size/type checks, and maximum comment lengths.
The system shall handle exceptional cases such as overlapping module sessions, duplicate enrollments, and cross-listed modules by allowing Administrators to resolve conflicts.
The system shall provide a visible status indicator for each absence (e.g., pending justification, justified, rejected, waived).
The system shall allow Teachers to add provisional notes on an absence that are visible to Administrative Staff and Referring Teachers but not editable by Students.
The system shall allow Students to view their full absence history with details, justification status, administrative comments, and attached documents.
The system shall provide role-based access control so Teachers can only view absences for students in their assigned modules and Referring Teachers can view and manage students/modules assigned to them.
The system shall provide Administrators and Administrative Staff with the ability to create custom absence reason codes and map them to justification policies.
The system shall provide an API (RESTful or GraphQL) for integration with external Student Information Systems (SIS), timetable systems, and single sign-on (SSO) providers.
The system shall support single sign-on (SSO) integration (SAML, OAuth2/OpenID Connect) and fall back to local authentication when SSO is unavailable.
The system shall provide configurable session timeout and secure session management with automatic logout after inactivity.
The system shall enforce strong password policies (minimum length, complexity, prevention of common passwords) and support password reset via secure tokens.
The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS and encrypt sensitive data at rest according to institutional policy.
The system shall implement role-based authorization checks on the server side for every sensitive API and page.
The system shall sanitize and escape all user-provided text to prevent XSS attacks and use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.
The system shall implement rate limiting and abuse protections on public endpoints (login, registration, API) to mitigate brute-force and denial-of-service attacks.
The system shall provide administrator-configurable data retention and automatic anonymization or deletion of records to comply with privacy regulations.
The system shall maintain backups of the database and uploaded files on a schedule defined by the institution, with documented restore procedures and testing.
The system shall log security events and provide an alerting mechanism to Administrators for suspicious activity (multiple failed logins, bulk deletions).
The system shall be resilient to failures and provide high availability with a target uptime SLA (e.g., 99.5%) and documented maintenance windows.
The system shall provide performance targets such that common pages (dashboards, absence lists, search results) respond within two seconds under normal load.
The system shall be horizontally scalable to support the institution’s peak concurrent user load with an initial minimum capacity for 500 concurrent users.
The system shall provide a mobile-responsive user interface that works on common browsers and screen sizes.
The system shall comply with accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) so that users with disabilities can navigate and use all core features.
The system shall support multiple languages/localization for UI text, date/time formats, and notifications.
The system shall provide contextual help and user documentation accessible in the application for each role (Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, Administrative Staff).
The system shall provide a searchable activity history for each student showing module attendance, justifications, administrative actions, and exports.
The system shall implement fine-grained permissions so Administrators can grant or revoke capabilities to other Administrators and staff.
The system shall provide a reconciliation tool to detect and report inconsistencies between module rosters and recorded absences.
The system shall allow Administrators and Administrative Staff to schedule recurring module sessions (timetabled classes) and auto-generate session entries for Teachers to mark attendance.
The system shall allow Teachers to mark attendance for an entire session (present/absent) with the ability to override individual student entries.
The system shall allow partial attendances (e.g., half-session) to be recorded with a standard notation and associated impact on absence counts.
The system shall allow Administrators to define and configure business rules for absence counting (e.g., number of lates equals one absence) and apply those rules to alerts and reports.
The system shall provide a workflow for dispute resolution where Students or Teachers can request review of an absence record and Administrators can record outcomes.
The system shall provide email templates for institutional notices related to absences that Administrators can customize.
The system shall provide time zone handling and validation to ensure absence timestamps are accurate across regionally distributed users.
The system shall handle daylight saving time transitions correctly when recording and displaying session times and absences.
The system shall provide role-specific dashboards: global metrics for Administrators, module and student metrics for Referring Teachers and Teachers, and personal metrics for Students.
The system shall provide bulk import and bulk edit capabilities for absence records with validation and error reporting.
The system shall provide a permissions-based audit report that can be exported to show who accessed or modified sensitive student attendance data.
The system shall require explicit consent or institutional acknowledgment for storage and processing of student personal data during onboarding.
The system shall mask or redact personally identifiable information in exports when required by privacy settings or roles.
The system shall support configurable retention and archival policies for attachments (medical documents) with restricted access and audit trail.
The system shall provide an administrative interface to configure global and per-module notification preferences and thresholds.
The system shall perform server-side validation to prevent creation of absences dated in the far future beyond a configurable horizon.
The system shall provide conflict detection and resolution when multiple Teachers attempt to edit the same absence record concurrently.
The system shall provide automated daily or weekly summary emails to Referring Teachers and Teachers listing new absences and outstanding justifications.
The system shall include unit, integration, and end-to-end test coverage for core business logic including authorization, absence counting, and justification workflows.
The system shall provide a sandbox or staging environment for Administrators to test configuration changes before applying them to production.
The system shall support a rollback mechanism for configuration changes affecting absence counting rules and alert thresholds.
The system shall maintain backward compatibility of APIs and data exports for at least two major releases or provide migration tooling.
The system shall provide configurable retention for deleted records in a soft-delete state and a permanent deletion process with administrator confirmation.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to flag absences as sensitive and restrict visibility to a subset of roles when necessary.
The system shall enable Administrators to configure which roles can create, edit, delete, justify, or export absence records.
The system shall provide a mechanism to embargo or lock a reporting period so that attendance data for that period cannot be modified without explicit Administrator override.
The system shall validate and normalize student enrollments against the module roster at the time an absence is recorded and record exceptions when mismatches occur.
The system shall provide a notification and approval workflow for Referring Teachers to enroll or remove students from modules when bulk changes are made.
The system shall provide an unobtrusive in-app notification center that stores recent notifications and marks them read/unread.
The system shall implement pagination and export limits to prevent extremely large single exports without Administrator confirmation.
The system shall include a dashboard widget or report that highlights students at risk based on configurable rules combining absence counts, trend, and teacher notes.
The system shall ensure that all actions that affect student records show an explicit confirmation dialog indicating the impact on absence tallies and alerts.
The system shall provide a configurable retention period for audit logs and a mechanism for secure long-term archival.
The system shall provide role-specific training materials and an administrative operations manual accessible within the application.
The system shall implement graceful degradation of non-critical features during partial outages while preserving core attendance recording functionality.
The system shall support onboarding data synchronization from existing institutional directories and provide conflict-resolution reporting for mismatches.
The system shall provide fine-grained logging for troubleshooting with options for Administrators to enable verbose logs for short diagnostic windows.
The system shall support legal and regulatory reporting needs by allowing Administrators to generate time-bounded reports of absences, justifications, and administrative decisions.
The system shall provide an interface to configure business hours and holiday calendars so that absence recording and notifications respect institutional schedules.
The system shall permit Administrators to delegate limited administrative privileges to Administrative Staff with scoped access to specific modules or student groups.
The system shall provide built-in data validation and warnings when importing or synchronizing rosters to avoid duplicate or inconsistent enrollments.
The system shall store all timestamps in UTC internally and present localized timestamps to users based on their profile settings.
The system shall allow exporting anonymized datasets for research or analytics while preserving individual privacy.
The system shall maintain consistent numbering or identifiers for modules, sessions, and absence events for traceability across systems.
The system shall support an escalation workflow for chronic absenteeism where automatic referrals or alerts are generated after configurable thresholds and approvals.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to bulk-apply a single justification reason to multiple absences (e.g., campus-wide event).
The system shall include automated validation that prevents marking a student as present and absent for the same session.
The system shall notify Referring Teachers when a student in their care exceeds an absence threshold even if the student’s module teachers have not yet acted.
The system shall provide a printable view of module attendance rosters for Teachers and Referring Teachers.
The system shall provide translation-ready UI strings and a mechanism for Administrators to supply institution-specific terminology (e.g., "module" vs "course").
The system shall provide an administrative audit that lists current configuration that affects absence counting and alerting for transparency and compliance.
The system shall support scheduled automated tasks (cron-style) for sending reminders, generating reports, and archiving old records with logs of successful/failed runs.

The system shall provide secure authentication for all users (Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, Administrative Staff) using username and password.
The system shall support role-based access control (RBAC) enforcing distinct permissions for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.
The system shall allow Administrators to create new user accounts with role assignment and mandatory profile fields (name, email, unique ID, role).
The system shall allow Administrators to edit all user profile fields and role assignments.
The system shall allow Administrators to deactivate, reactivate, and delete user accounts, with an option for soft-delete (recoverable) and hard-delete (permanent) and configurable retention period before hard-delete.
The system shall prevent deletion of users who have dependent attendance records unless the Administrator confirms deletion or reassigns/archives those records.
The system shall maintain an audit log of all user account creations, modifications, deactivations, and deletions recording who, when, and what changed.
The system shall allow Administrators to create teaching modules with a unique module identifier, name, description, academic period, and associated metadata (credits, schedule template).
The system shall allow Administrators to edit module data and maintain a version/history log of changes.
The system shall allow Administrators to delete modules with a configurable policy (soft-delete, cascade archive, or prevent deletion if attendance exists) and require Administrator confirmation before destructive actions.
The system shall allow Administrators to associate each module with one or more Referring Teachers.
The system shall permit Administrators to assign or reassign Referring Teachers to modules individually or in bulk.
The system shall validate that Referring Teachers assigned to a module exist in the global user database.
The system shall enable Administrators to create, maintain, and delete groups (cohorts/classes) and assign students and teachers to groups.
The system shall maintain a central, global database of teachers and students managed by Administrators.
The system shall support import of user, group, and module data in common formats (CSV, XLSX) with duplicate detection and import validation reports.
The system shall provide export of user, group, module, and attendance data in CSV and PDF formats with configurable columns.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add Teachers and Students to modules or groups only if those users already exist in the global database.
The system shall present Referring Teachers with a searchable list of existing users to link to modules or groups.
The system shall prevent Referring Teachers from creating new global user accounts; only Administrators may create new users.
The system shall allow Teachers to mark attendance for a given module session and for individual students (present, absent, late, excused, partial) and to record the date and time of the session.
The system shall allow Teachers to report an absence for any student enrolled in one of their modules.
The system shall allow Teachers to add a free-text comment when they record an absence for a student.
The system shall store each attendance record with: student ID, module ID, teacher ID (who recorded), session date/time, status, comment, and timestamp of creation and last modification.
The system shall allow Teachers to edit or delete attendance records they created within a configurable time window and shall record edits in the audit log.
The system shall allow Administrators and Administrative Staff to edit attendance records and to add/modify the official reason for absence.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter the official reason for an absence and mark it as justified or unjustified.
The system shall support a configurable set of absence reasons (illness, medical appointment, family emergency, school activity, other) and allow free-text custom reasons.
The system shall allow Students to view their own attendance records, comments, and justification status.
The system shall allow Students to submit an absence justification request with free-text explanation and attach supporting documents (images, PDFs) with configurable size and file-type limits.
The system shall notify Administrative Staff and the Referring Teacher when a Student submits a justification request for an absence.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to upload and attach supporting documents to attendance records.
The system shall maintain an immutable history of status changes for each absence (reported -> justification submitted -> reason entered -> justified/unjustified) with timestamps and actor IDs.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to view the list of absences for all students enrolled in any of their modules.
The system shall allow Referring Teachers to view module-level and student-level attendance reports for modules they are assigned to.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff and Administrators to view all student absences across the institution.
The system shall present absences grouped by module and by student with interactive filters (date range, status, reason, teacher, group).
The system shall offer aggregation views (counts per student, counts per module, counts per date range) and support sorting by count, name, and date.
The system shall provide a configurable alert page that lists students with more than three unjustified absences and shall allow filtering by module, group, and date range.
The system shall make the alert page visible to Teaching Staff (Teachers and Referring Teachers) and to Administrative Staff and Administrators.
The system shall treat "unjustified absence" as an absence record where Administrative Staff has not marked the absence as justified and/or has marked it explicitly as unjustified, subject to configurable business rules.
The system shall provide a configurable threshold for the number of unjustified absences that trigger alerts and define whether the threshold is cumulative or within a configurable period (e.g., per semester, last 30 days).
The system shall send automated notifications (email and optional SMS) to relevant Teachers, Referring Teachers, Administrative Staff, and optionally Students when a student exceeds the unjustified absence threshold.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to acknowledge alerts and add notes or remediation plans to the student's attendance record.
The system shall allow bulk marking of attendance for a class session (mark all present, then mark absentees) and save change sets atomically.
The system shall support partial attendance statuses (late arrival with minutes late, early leave with minutes, present for only part) and reflect them in aggregated absence/attendance counts according to configurable rules.
The system shall prevent Teachers from recording attendance for modules and sessions they are not authorized for.
The system shall allow scheduling of module sessions (date/time, location) and associate attendance records with a scheduled session or ad-hoc session.
The system shall expose a calendar or session list to Teachers and Referring Teachers showing upcoming sessions they are responsible for.
The system shall allow Teachers to retrospectively record attendance for past sessions within a configurable limit and flag retrospective entries as such.
The system shall support conflict detection (e.g., two sessions same time for the same room or teacher) and warn Administrators when creating schedules.
The system shall provide a search function for users, modules, groups, and attendance records with filters and saved queries.
The system shall provide printable and downloadable attendance reports per student, per module, per group, and per period, with configurable formats.
The system shall provide role-appropriate dashboards summarizing key metrics: for Teachers — recent absences and alerts for their modules; for Referring Teachers — module summaries and enrollment actions; for Administrative Staff — institution-wide absence statistics; for Students — personal attendance summary.
The system shall provide an API (REST or GraphQL) for integration with third-party systems (student information systems, timetable systems) with token-based authentication and role-based access.
The system shall provide import/update operations for enrollment lists to allow Administrators to sync students and teachers with external systems.
The system shall validate all inputs and prevent invalid data entry (missing required fields, invalid date ranges, duplicate enrollments).
The system shall enforce uniqueness constraints for user email and unique ID and for module identifiers.
The system shall display clear, contextual error messages for validation failures to end users.
The system shall send email notifications for critical actions: account created, password reset, attendance recorded (optional per user preferences), justification status changed, and alert thresholds exceeded.
The system shall allow users to set notification preferences (email on/off, daily digest, push notifications if supported).
The system shall implement session management with automatic logout after configurable inactivity timeout and prevent concurrent sessions if configured.
The system shall provide secure password-reset mechanisms with multi-step verification (email token) and enforce password strength policies.
The system shall support optional two-factor authentication for privileged roles (Administrators and Administrative Staff).
The system shall provide a user-friendly interface that is responsive and usable on desktop and mobile browsers.
The system shall conform to accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) to support users with disabilities.
The system shall offer UI localization and language selection for all visible text with default language set and ability to add translations.
The system shall implement role-based visibility so students see only their own data and teachers see only modules/groups they are assigned to unless given expanded permissions.
The system shall implement fine-grained permission controls for Administrators to grant temporary or limited elevated privileges (e.g., escalate a Teacher to Referring Teacher for a module).
The system shall provide an administrative interface for configuring business rules: absence reason codes, justification workflow, unjustified absence threshold, retrospective recording window, file upload policies, notification templates.
The system shall provide activity/audit logs for all attendance record actions (create, update, delete, justify) including actor, timestamps, previous values, and IP address.
The system shall allow Administrators to search and filter audit logs and export them for compliance.
The system shall store uploaded documents securely and link them to the related attendance record, with configurable retention and deletion policies.
The system shall implement file scanning for malware on uploaded attachments and block unsafe file types.
The system shall provide data retention and archival policies configurable by Administrator to archive old attendance records and associated documents.
The system shall encrypt sensitive data in transit (TLS) and store sensitive fields (passwords, personal identifiers) encrypted at rest.
The system shall comply with applicable privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR) including consent records, data subject access requests, and the ability to export or erase a student's personal data in accordance with policy and retention rules.
The system shall implement role-appropriate data masking in exports and on-screen for non-authorized users.
The system shall enforce rate limiting and input throttling to protect against automated abuse.
The system shall be designed for high availability with defined uptime SLA (configurable by deployment) and graceful degradation of non-critical features during partial outages.
The system shall respond to UI requests within acceptable performance thresholds (e.g., pages load within 2 seconds under normal load) and provide pagination for large result sets.
The system shall scale horizontally to support growing numbers of users and concurrent attendance operations.
The system shall provide scheduled automated backups of the database and file storage with configurable retention and tested disaster recovery procedures.
The system shall support transactional integrity for bulk attendance operations so that incomplete operations are rolled back on error.
The system shall prevent race conditions when multiple users edit the same attendance record, using optimistic locking or edit locks, and present merge/conflict resolution prompts.
The system shall provide comprehensive automated unit and integration tests for core attendance and user-management functionality, and support deployment pipelines.
The system shall provide data validation for date/time entries with timezone awareness and clear handling of daylight saving transitions.
The system shall guard against fraudulent justification submissions by logging submitter IP and device and allowing Administrative Staff to request additional verification.
The system shall provide an interface for Administrative Staff to bulk-justify or bulk-unjustify absences with audit recording.
The system shall allow Administrators to reassign Referring Teachers or Teachers for a module and automatically notify affected users.
The system shall allow Administrators to merge duplicate user accounts and merge associated attendance records with conflict resolution and audit trail.
The system shall handle orphaned attendance records (e.g., user deleted) by offering options to reassign, anonymize, or archive while preserving compliance.
The system shall provide role-based printable notifications or letters (e.g., warning letters for excessive unjustified absences) with placeholders for student and attendance data.
The system shall provide a configurable retention period and archival process for alert states and resolved alerts.
The system shall allow Teachers to filter attendance lists by status (present/absent/justified/unjustified/late) and to search within lists.
The system shall provide inline help, tooltips, and a user manual accessible from the UI for all roles.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to import external justifications (doctor’s notes) and attach them to corresponding absence records.
The system shall implement secure API keys and OAuth2 token mechanisms for integrations with external systems and revoke tokens on demand.
The system shall provide notifications and logs for integration failures and data synchronization errors with retry and alert mechanisms.
The system shall allow configurable business hours and contact details for modules and staff to be displayed on relevant pages.
The system shall provide a sandbox/test environment for Administrators and developers to validate changes without affecting production data.
The system shall support browser compatibility across modern browsers and degrade gracefully for unsupported browsers.
The system shall record IP addresses and device/user-agent strings for security-relevant events (login, password reset, attendance modification).
The system shall allow filtering of alerts and reports by academic period, semester, or custom date ranges.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to export attendance lists for a session in printable format for in-class use.
The system shall allow Administrators to configure what roles receive which notifications and the cadence (real-time, daily digest).
