The system shall provide a configurable retention / purge schedule for uploaded files and logs with Administrator override.
The system shall provide clear on-screen confirmation and undo capability for destructive actions within a short time frame.
The system shall support multi-tenancy or logical partitioning if required for departmental separation, with strict data isolation.
The system shall include mechanisms for reporting bugs and requesting features from within the UI and track those reports for Administrators.
The system shall provide analytics dashboards for Administrators showing trends (absence rates over time, modules with highest absence, student risk profiles).
The system shall allow configuration of which absence statuses count toward unjustified absence thresholds (e.g., late vs absent).
The system shall provide role-specific training material and onboarding flows for first-time users.
The system shall ensure all user-facing text (including error messages) is modifiable by Administrators and translatable.
The system shall implement secure deployment practices and regular security patching guidance for the hosting environment.
The system shall provide an emergency contact and override mechanism for Administrators to perform immediate global actions (e.g., freeze records, disable logins) with audit trail.

The system shall provide role-based authentication for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff accounts.
The system shall allow users to register accounts subject to administrator approval or pre-registration by the administrator.
The system shall enforce unique usernames and unique institutional email addresses for all user accounts.
The system shall require secure passwords that meet configurable complexity rules and shall support password reset via validated email.
The system shall support multi-factor authentication for administrator and administrative staff accounts.
The system shall store all user roles and permissions in an auditable, role-based access control (RBAC) model.
The Administrator shall be able to create, read, update, and delete ("CRUD") teaching modules.
The Administrator shall be able to assign one or more Referring Teachers to each module.
The Administrator shall be able to associate existing Teachers and Students to modules or remove those associations.
The system shall enforce that a Referring Teacher or Teacher may only be assigned to modules if their user account exists in the global database.
The Administrator shall be able to create, read, update, and delete user accounts for all roles.
The Administrator shall be able to define and manage module metadata including module code, title, description, scheduled sessions, academic term, and capacity.
The Administrator shall be able to configure module schedules (dates, start/end times, recurring patterns) and associate sessions with modules.
The system shall maintain referential integrity between modules, users, enrollments, and absence records.
The global database of Teachers and Students shall be centrally managed by the Administrator and allow search, filtering, and bulk operations.
The Referring Teacher shall be able to add existing Teachers to a module if those Teachers already exist in the global database.
The Referring Teacher shall be able to add existing Students to a module if those Students already exist in the global database.
The Referring Teacher shall be prevented from adding Teachers or Students who are not present in the global database and shall be offered a workflow to request administrator registration.
The system shall record module enrollments with enrollment date, enrollment source (Admin/Referring Teacher/import), and enrollment status (active/inactive/withdrawn).
A Teacher shall be able to report an absence for themselves for a specific module session and specify the date and time of the absence.
When reporting an absence, a Teacher shall be able to add a free-text comment limited to a configurable maximum length.
The system shall record each absence with a unique identifier, reporting user, reporting role, timestamp, associated module and session (if any), and original comment.
A Teacher shall be able to view the list of absences for all Students enrolled in any module in which that Teacher is assigned.
A Teacher shall be able to filter and sort student absence lists by module, student, date range, justification status, reason, and severity.
Administrative Staff shall have access to view, search, filter, and export all student absences across all modules.
Administrative Staff shall be able to enter or update the official reason for any absence and mark absences as justified, unjustified, or pending.
Students shall be able to view their own absence records including date, module, reporting teacher, comments, official reason, and justification status.
Students shall be able to submit justifications for their absences that include free-text explanation and optional supporting document uploads (PDF, JPG, PNG).
Administrative Staff shall be able to review student-submitted justifications, attach decisions, and mark absences as justified or rejected with comments.
The system shall support attachment storage for supporting documents and enforce file type and file size limits configurable by the Administrator.
The system shall notify relevant parties by configurable channels (email, in-app notification, SMS if enabled) when an absence is reported, when a student submits a justification, and when a justification is accepted or rejected.
The system shall maintain an auditable history (who, what, when) for each absence record and for all changes to status, reasons, comments, and attachments.
Absences shall be groupable and viewable aggregated by module and by student.
The system shall provide summary dashboards showing counts of absences by module, by student, by date range, and by justification status.
The system shall provide a dedicated page listing students who have more than three unjustified absences and allow filtering by module, term, and date range.
The threshold for the alert page ("more than three unjustified absences") shall be configurable by the Administrator and may be applied per term, per rolling period, or per module.
The system shall automatically compute counts of unjustified absences per student and update alerts in real time upon status changes.
The system shall notify the assigned Teachers and Referring Teachers when a student exceeds the configured unjustified absence threshold.
The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to acknowledge or dismiss alerts with an auditable reason.
The system shall provide role-restricted dashboards: Administrators with system-wide controls, Administrative Staff with full absence handling privileges, Referring Teachers with module-level enrolment controls, Teachers with reporting and viewing privileges, and Students with personal records access.
The system shall provide search functionality across users, modules, and absences with support for partial matches and multiple filters.
The system shall support pagination and efficient querying for large datasets and expose export functionality (CSV, XLSX, PDF) for reports.
The system shall provide an API for integration with external Student Information Systems (SIS) for user and enrollment synchronization, with authentication and authorization.
The system shall support bulk import and export of users, module assignments, and historical absences via CSV with validation reports for errors.
The system shall prevent duplicate absence reports for the same user, same module session, and same timestamp and shall warn users of potential duplicates with an override only for authorized roles.
The system shall allow editing and deletion of absence records only by authorized roles and shall record edits/deletions in the audit trail.
When a module or user is deleted, the system shall provide options to cascade delete, anonymize, or retain associated absence records, and the chosen behavior shall be confirmed and auditable.
The system shall provide configurable retention and archival policies for absence records and attachments in compliance with institutional rules.
The system shall validate all inputs including dates, file uploads, numeric fields, and enforce referential constraints on module and user references.
The system shall support concurrent access and optimistic locking to prevent lost updates when multiple users modify the same absence or enrollment records simultaneously.
The system shall expose role-based activity logs showing sign-in events, actions performed, and IP addresses for administrative review.
The system shall enforce session timeouts and automatic logout after a configurable period of inactivity.
The system shall provide localized UI strings and support for at least one configurable language with easy addition of more languages.
The system shall be responsive and usable on desktop, tablet, and mobile web browsers.
The system shall meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards for screen-reader compatibility, keyboard navigation, and color contrast.
The system shall encrypt sensitive personal data at rest and in transit using industry-standard algorithms (e.g., TLS for transit and AES-256 for storage).
The system shall enforce least-privilege access controls to sensitive fields such as medical justification notes.
The system shall provide role-based data visibility masks for confidential information when required by policy.
The system shall maintain backups of the database and attachments at a configurable cadence and allow restoration to a point-in-time within the backup retention window.
The system shall provide audit logs that cannot be tampered with by non-administrative users and shall be retained according to retention policies.
The system shall perform input validation and return user-friendly error messages for invalid or missing required fields.
The system shall provide configurable email templates for notifications and allow previews before sending.
The system shall throttle or rate-limit automated notifications to prevent spamming recipients.
The system shall provide configurable business rules for how justifications are processed (e.g., auto-approve certain document types, require manual approval).
The system shall support configurable absence types such as excused, unexcused, tardy/late, and support mapping rules by Administrative Staff.
The system shall allow Teachers to view historical attendance trends and charts for individual students and modules.
The system shall permit Administrative Staff to generate and download periodic reports (daily/weekly/monthly/termly) on absences segmented by module, student, teacher, and justification status.
The system shall provide exportable audit-ready reports with timestamps and user identifiers for official record keeping.
The system shall provide fine-grained permission controls for which roles can mark absence justification, edit reasons, delete records, and export data.
The system shall support configurable notification recipient lists per module (e.g., module teachers, referring teachers, administrative staff).
The system shall allow students to contest or appeal justification rejections through an internal workflow routed to Administrative Staff and Referring Teacher as configured.
The system shall support escalation rules that notify higher-level administrators if a student's unjustified absence count exceeds escalating thresholds.
The system shall prevent teachers from falsely reporting absences via review, requiring confirmation for reporting for others and logging originator IP and device.
The system shall provide the ability for Referring Teachers to request administrator action to register new users not found in the database and track those requests.
The system shall track academic term and support queries scoped to a specific academic term or date range.
The system shall validate that reported absences reference a valid scheduled session for the module when session data exists, and allow free-date reporting otherwise.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to set default absence reasons and maintain a taxonomy of standardized reasons.
The system shall allow custom free-text reasons and map them to standardized categories where possible.
The system shall provide confirmation dialogs for destructive operations (delete, irreversible changes) and require explicit confirmation by authorized users.
The system shall provide user activity notifications in an in-application inbox as well as optional email/SMS.
The system shall provide a configurable dashboard widget for Teachers and Referring Teachers showing recent absences, pending justifications, and alerts.
The system shall ensure sub-second response times for common queries under normal load and define acceptable SLAs (e.g., 95th percentile page load <2s for dashboards).
The system shall scale to support the institution's maximum expected number of concurrent users with horizontal scaling options for application and database tiers.
The system shall maintain high availability with planned maintenance windows announced in advance and a target uptime of 99.5% per term.
The system shall implement secure coding practices and regular vulnerability scanning and address critical vulnerabilities within a defined SLA.
The system shall log and monitor errors and performance metrics and provide alerting to system administrators for outage conditions.
The system shall provide clear help text, inline field validation, and a searchable help/FAQ section for users.
The system shall provide a role-based administrative UI to configure business rules, notification thresholds, file size limits, retention periods, and absence types.
The system shall support data export in machine-readable formats and provide an audit of exports and which user performed them.
The system shall enforce privacy controls to comply with applicable data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR), including data subject access requests, the right to be forgotten, and export of personal data.
The system shall provide retainment and deletion workflows that ensure legal hold exceptions can be applied to specific records.
The system shall include unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for core workflows including absence reporting, justification, approval, and alert generation.
The system shall provide an administrative audit trail viewer that supports filtering by date range, user, action type, and affected records.
The system shall implement transactional integrity so that multi-step operations (e.g., bulk imports, enrollment changes) are atomic and can be rolled back in case of errors.
The system shall protect against common web threats including CSRF, XSS, SQL injection, and implement CSP and security headers.
The system shall allow configuration of business hours and holiday calendars so absence reporting and alerts can account for non-instructional days.
The system shall support timezone-aware date/time handling and display times according to user preferences or institution default timezone.
The system shall provide an impersonation feature for Administrators to securely assume another user's view for troubleshooting with explicit logging and approval.
The system shall provide a configurable escalation and notification retry policy for failed message deliveries.
The system shall provide mechanisms to reconcile and merge duplicate user accounts while preserving associated absence history and audit trails.
The system shall validate and enforce module capacity constraints if an institution requires enrollment limits.
The system shall allow teachers to mark a student as present, absent, tardy, or excused for specific sessions and optionally record duration of absence or lateness.
The system shall provide a bulk absence reporting UI for Teachers and Administrative Staff for cases like field trips or large-scale events.
The system shall provide a mechanism for Teachers to attach session-level notes (e.g., class canceled) that affect absence interpretation.
The system shall display computed statistics such as percent attendance per module and per student for configured periods.
The system shall support scheduled automated reports to be emailed to selected recipients on configurable schedules.
The system shall provide role-appropriate dashboards and restrict access to sensitive aggregation data to authorized roles.
The system shall implement graceful error handling with clear recovery steps and error identifiers for support.
The system shall provide a secure admin operations dashboard to track system health, backups, and recent exports.
The system shall support versioning of module definitions so historical sessions and absences consistently reference the module state at the time of the event.
The system shall support configurable retention and archival storage tiers to optimize cost for large attachment volumes.
The system shall require explicit consent for communications and store user communication preferences.
The system shall provide integration points for single sign-on (SSO) via SAML or OAuth2 for institutional identity providers.
The system shall include rate limits and abuse detection for public-facing endpoints to protect against denial-of-service attempts.
The system shall provide data validation and human-readable import error reporting for bulk uploads, with the ability to correct and re-run failed records.
The system shall provide a consented audit and reporting mechanism when Administrative Staff modifies sensitive justification data.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to attach internal-only notes to absence records visible only to staff and administrators.
The system shall allow configurable retention of anonymized or aggregated data for analytical purposes while protecting personally identifiable information.
The system shall provide a dispute-resolution workflow preserving all communications and actions taken with timestamps and actors.
The system shall include documentation for administrators, teachers, and students describing workflows, permissions, and contact points for help.
The system shall provide a sandbox or staging environment for testing configuration changes without affecting production data.
The system shall enforce sensible defaults on all configurable settings and provide role-based safeguards to prevent misconfiguration that could lead to data loss.
The system shall support periodic data synchronization tasks and provide monitoring and reporting on synchronization success/failure.
The system shall allow administrators to configure whether teachers may directly justify student absences or only submit comments and leave justification to Administrative Staff.
The system shall allow Administrative Staff to tag absences with follow-up actions (e.g., parental contact required) and track completion of those actions.
The system shall provide secure deletion workflows that permanently remove files from storage within the constraints of retention and legal hold rules.
The system shall enforce provenance on every absence or justification action by recording the source device, IP, and user agent where feasible.
The system shall provide exportable compliance reports summarizing retention actions, deletions, and legal holds.
